name: Age
description: Patient age at the time of examination. Dry eye prevalence generally increases with age, especially after 50, due to decreased tear production.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Age has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.005, p=0.4571). Age shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.4662).

name: Gender
description: Biological sex. Women are 2× more likely to develop dry eye due to hormonal fluctuations (menopause, pregnancy, oral contraceptives).
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Gender has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=5.3, Cramér's V=0.016, p=0.0218).

name: Sleep duration
description: Total hours of sleep per night. Insufficient sleep (<6h) reduces tear film stability and increases ocular surface inflammation.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Sleep duration has a weak negative correlation with dry eye (r=-0.001, p=0.9016). Sleep duration shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.9055).

name: Sleep quality
description: Self-reported sleep quality on a 1–5 scale (1=very poor, 5=excellent). Poor sleep quality correlates with increased inflammatory markers affecting the ocular surface.
insight: Sleep quality has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=1.4, Cramér's V=0.008, p=0.8447).

name: Stress level
description: Self-reported stress on a 1–5 scale (1=low, 5=high). Chronic stress triggers cortisol release, which disrupts meibomian gland function and tear composition.
insight: Stress level has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=2.8, Cramér's V=0.012, p=0.5854).

name: BP_Systolic
description: Systolic blood pressure — the upper number. Hypertension can impair microvascular circulation to the lacrimal gland, reducing tear production.
insight: BP_Systolic has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.007, p=0.3026). BP_Systolic shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.3015).

name: BP_Diastolic
description: Diastolic blood pressure — the lower number. Elevated diastolic BP reflects increased peripheral resistance, which may affect orbital blood supply.
insight: BP_Diastolic has a weak negative correlation with dry eye (r=-0.005, p=0.4751). BP_Diastolic shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.4735).

name: Heart rate
description: Resting heart rate. Autonomic nervous system imbalance (reflected in HR) can affect lacrimal gland secretion via parasympathetic pathways.
insight: Heart rate has a weak negative correlation with dry eye (r=-0.002, p=0.7683). Heart rate shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.7635).

name: Daily steps
description: Average daily step count. Physical activity improves systemic circulation and has anti-inflammatory effects that may protect the ocular surface.
insight: Daily steps has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.006, p=0.4232). Daily steps shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.4236).

name: Physical activity
description: Self-rated physical activity level (1–5 scale). Regular exercise is associated with improved tear film quality through reduced systemic inflammation.
insight: Physical activity has a weak negative correlation with dry eye (r=-0.003, p=0.6419). Physical activity shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.6410).

name: Height
description: Patient height in centimeters. Used to derive BMI. Not directly linked to dry eye but part of the metabolic profile.
relevance_label: ⚪ No direct clinical link — auxiliary variable
insight: Height has a weak negative correlation with dry eye (r=-0.006, p=0.3627). Height shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.3606).

name: Weight
description: Patient weight in kilograms. Obesity (high weight) is linked to chronic low-grade inflammation that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
insight: Weight has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.002, p=0.7290). Weight shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.7253).

name: BMI
description: Body Mass Index (Weight/Height²). Obese individuals (BMI ≥30) have higher rates of meibomian gland dysfunction due to metabolic syndrome-related inflammation.
insight: BMI has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.007, p=0.3106). BMI shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: not significant (p=0.4926).

name: Sleep disorder
description: Diagnosed sleep disorder (Y/N). Sleep apnea and insomnia are associated with a 2–3× increase in dry eye risk through floppy eyelid syndrome and reduced REM-phase tear film maintenance.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Sleep disorder has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.1, Cramér's V=0.003, p=0.7222). Patients with sleep disorder have a higher dry eye rate (65.3% vs 65.1%). Odds ratio: 1.01.

name: Wake up during night
description: Frequent nighttime awakening (Y/N). Disrupted sleep architecture reduces the nocturnal tear film regeneration cycle, leaving eyes more vulnerable in the morning.
insight: Wake up during night has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.0, Cramér's V=0.000, p=0.9763). Patients with wake up during night have a lower dry eye rate (65.2% vs 65.2%). Odds ratio: 1.00.

name: Feel sleepy during day
description: Daytime drowsiness (Y/N). A proxy for sleep insufficiency — correlates with reduced blink rate and incomplete blinks during drowsy periods.
insight: Feel sleepy during day has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.4, Cramér's V=0.005, p=0.5131). Patients with feel sleepy during day have a higher dry eye rate (65.4% vs 65.0%). Odds ratio: 1.02.

name: Caffeine consumption
description: Regular caffeine intake (Y/N). Caffeine has a mild diuretic effect but studies show it may paradoxically increase tear secretion via adenosine A2 receptor stimulation.
insight: Caffeine consumption has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.1, Cramér's V=0.003, p=0.6999). Patients with caffeine consumption have a higher dry eye rate (65.3% vs 65.0%). Odds ratio: 1.01.

name: Alcohol consumption
description: Regular alcohol consumption (Y/N). Alcohol is a vasodilator and diuretic — chronic use can cause systemic dehydration affecting tear osmolarity and volume.
insight: Alcohol consumption has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=2.5, Cramér's V=0.011, p=0.1147). Patients with alcohol consumption have a higher dry eye rate (65.7% vs 64.6%). Odds ratio: 1.05.

name: Smoking
description: Active smoker (Y/N). Cigarette smoke is a direct ocular surface irritant and contains >7,000 chemicals that destabilize the tear film lipid layer.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Smoking has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.5, Cramér's V=0.005, p=0.4777). Patients with smoking have a lower dry eye rate (64.9% vs 65.4%). Odds ratio: 0.98.

name: Medical issue
description: Pre-existing medical condition (Y/N). Conditions like diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, and thyroid disease are strongly linked to dry eye through inflammatory and autoimmune pathways.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Medical issue has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.0, Cramér's V=0.001, p=0.8903). Patients with medical issue have a lower dry eye rate (65.1% vs 65.2%). Odds ratio: 0.99.

name: Ongoing medication
description: Currently taking medication (Y/N). Many medications (antihistamines, antidepressants, beta-blockers, diuretics) have anticholinergic effects that reduce tear secretion.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Ongoing medication has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.3, Cramér's V=0.004, p=0.5636). Patients with ongoing medication have a lower dry eye rate (65.0% vs 65.4%). Odds ratio: 0.98.

name: Smart device before bed
description: Uses smartphone/tablet within 1 hour of sleep (Y/N). Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin production and concentrated screen fixation reduces blink rate by 60%.
insight: Smart device before bed has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=3.5, Cramér's V=0.013, p=0.0616). Patients with smart device before bed have a lower dry eye rate (64.6% vs 65.8%). Odds ratio: 0.95.

name: Average screen time
description: Daily hours spent on digital screens. Prolonged screen use reduces blink rate from ~15/min to ~5/min, causing incomplete meibomian oil spread and rapid tear evaporation.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Average screen time has a weak positive correlation with dry eye (r=0.022, p=0.0015). Average screen time shows minimal statistical association with dry eye. Mann-Whitney U test: significant (p=0.0014).

name: Blue-light filter
description: Uses blue-light filtering glasses or screen filter (Y/N). Reduces high-energy visible (HEV) light that can cause retinal photoxicity and ocular surface inflammation.
insight: Blue-light filter has a weak association with dry eye (χ²=0.5, Cramér's V=0.005, p=0.4867). Patients with blue-light filter have a higher dry eye rate (65.4% vs 64.9%). Odds ratio: 1.02.

name: Discomfort Eye-strain
description: Experiences eye strain or discomfort (Y/N). A primary symptom of dry eye — caused by corneal nerve exposure when tear film breaks up. Often worst in the afternoon.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Discomfort Eye-strain has a moderate association with dry eye (χ²=241.0, Cramér's V=0.110, p=<0.001). Patients with discomfort eye-strain have a higher dry eye rate (70.4% vs 59.9%). Odds ratio: 1.59.

name: Redness in eye
description: Visible redness/bloodshot eyes (Y/N). Conjunctival hyperemia occurs when the tear film fails to protect the ocular surface, triggering vasodilation of conjunctival blood vessels.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Redness in eye has a moderate association with dry eye (χ²=213.4, Cramér's V=0.103, p=<0.001). Patients with redness in eye have a higher dry eye rate (70.2% vs 60.3%). Odds ratio: 1.55.

name: Itchiness/Irritation in eye
description: Eye itchiness or irritation (Y/N). Can indicate allergic conjunctivitis co-occurring with dry eye — a common overlap. Inflammation releases histamine causing the itch reflex.
relevance_label: 🔴 Strongly associated with dry eye in clinical literature
insight: Itchiness/Irritation in eye has a moderate association with dry eye (χ²=204.8, Cramér's V=0.101, p=<0.001). Patients with itchiness/irritation in eye have a higher dry eye rate (70.0% vs 60.4%). Odds ratio: 1.53.

name: Dry Eye Disease
description: The target variable — diagnosed Dry Eye Disease (Y/N). Defined as a multifactorial disease of the ocular surface characterized by tear film instability, hyperosmolarity, and neurosensory abnormalities.
insight: This is the target variable. 65.2% of patients have dry eye disease.
